summary: Frontend-only regression on /morse for new 5-preset feature. All 8 acceptance items PASS on the public preview (390x844). Verified: 5 numbered preset buttons + STOP button layout, empty short-tap opens editor without sending, long-press opens editor and saving activates the preset with repeat chips + 'Wdh. Baustein 2' label, finite 2× send drains queue and STOP toggles solid-red↔grey, infinite send keeps queue>0 with solid-red STOP and pressing STOP immediately returns queue-count to '0 in Warteschlange' AND the infinite loop remains terminated (queue stays 0 after 3s wait — no restart), live typing still enqueues + STOP goes red + Reset clears, presets (text + repeat='inf') persist via localStorage key funk_morse_presets_v1 across a full page reload. Only non-blocking web-only warnings observed: 'shadow*' and 'pointerEvents' deprecation (expected, called out in review).
critical_code_review_comments: useMorseSender.ts: unified queue design is clean — infiniteRef is reset by clear(), which guarantees the STOP semantics observed in Test D (queue stays 0, loop dead).
usePresets.ts: normalize() correctly validates repeat + text on load and falls back to defaults, so a corrupted JSON entry cannot crash the screen.

morse.tsx: presetRow is placed directly above the input (correct ordering per requirement a); STOP button visibility state (bg red rgb(244,67,54) vs grey rgb(18,18,18) @ opacity 0.45) confirmed via computed styles.
seed_data_creation: Cleared localStorage 'funk_morse_presets_v1' at start; created preset 2 = 'CQ DE' with repeat='inf' during test — persisted intentionally to prove persistence.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature is fully working. Preset localStorage key: funk_morse_presets_v1. STOP button color test: solid red = rgb(244,67,54) opacity 1; disabled/idle = rgb(18,18,18) opacity 0.45. Live-typing autoCapitalize cannot be validated via Playwright page.fill() (bypasses keyboard events); if verification is needed use page.keyboard.type() instead — the morse engine still transmits case-insensitively so this is a test-tool artifact, not an app bug.
